sections.paragraph:
Φρυγία, ας, ἡ Phrygia (Hom. et al.; ins, Joseph., SibOr; Tat. 39, 3.—On the use of the art. s. B-D-F §261, 6; as adj., s. Hemer, Acts 112 and 283, JTS n.s. 27, ’76, 122–26; also n.s. 28, ’77, 99–101) a large district in Central Asia Minor, whose boundaries varied considerably fr. time to time: Ac 2:10; 16:6; 18:23 (in the last two places w. ἡ Γαλατικὴ χώρα); 1 Ti subscr. (s. Πακατιανός); MPol 4.—CB (other publications by Ramsay in Harnack, Mission4 II 1924 p. 677, 3; WGasque, Sir William M. Ramsay ’66, 86–91); JWeiss, RE X 557ff; VSchultze, Altchristl. Städte u. Landschaften II 1, 1922; AGoltze, Kleinasien2 ’57; Pauly-W. XX 781–891; Kl. Pauly IV 822–26; BHHW III 1468f; WSchepelern, D. Montanismus u. d. phrygischen Kulte 1929.—M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

art. art. = article

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

adj. adj. = adjective

Hemer, Acts Hemer, Acts = CH., The Book of Acts in the Setting of Hellenistic History—List 6

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

  1. fr. = from

subscr. subscr. = subscription

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

CB Ramsay, CB = The Cities and Bishoprics of Phrygia

Ramsay Ramsay = WR.—Lists 3, 6

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1067
